title: Ranunculus triplex
taxonomy: Ranunculus triplex is classified under the kingdom Plantae. It belongs to the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. The species is a member of the Ranunculus genus, which includes various annual and perennial plants. Ranunculus triplex is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in areas with moist soil and full sun. The exact classification within the Ranunculus genus may vary depending on the source or current taxonomic research.
uses: Ranunculus triplex, a plant species, is not commonly known for its medicinal uses. However, its related species, such as the greater celandine (Chelidonium majus), have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The roots and leaves of the greater celandine contain compounds like berberine, which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
